description: American white supremacist and far-right hate group, founded in 1865 in Pulaski, Tennessee, by former Confederate soldiers. It has operated in three major iterations, using violence and intimidation to target African Americans, Jews, Catholics, and immigrants. The Klan is considered one of the earliest terrorist groups in the United States, with a history of lynching, political violence, and racial terror. Modern iterations are smaller but continue to promote white nationalism and anti-immigrant nativism.

country_links.evidence_quote: In Australia in the late 1990s, former One Nation party member Peter Coleman established Klan branches in several parts of the country...
country_links.context: Klan branches were established in several parts of Australia, including New South Wales and Victoria.

country_links.evidence_quote: In São Paulo, Brazil, the website of a group called itself the Imperial Klans of Brazil was shut down in 2003, and the group's leader was arrested.
country_links.context: A group calling itself the Imperial Klans of Brazil had a website shut down and its leader arrested.

country_links.evidence_quote: Klan organizers also operated in Canada, particularly in Saskatchewan in 1926 and 1928, where Klansmen denounced immigrants from Eastern Europe as a threat to Canada's 'Anglo-Saxon' heritage.
country_links.context: Klan organizers operated in Canada, particularly in Saskatchewan, to recruit members and denounce immigrants.

country_links.evidence_quote: In the 1920s, a small Klan presence also briefly existed in Shanghai.
country_links.context: A small Klan presence briefly existed in Shanghai in the 1920s.

country_links.evidence_quote: In Cuba, an organization known as the Ku Klux Klan Kubano targeted West Indian migrant workers and Afro-Cubans...
country_links.context: An organization known as the Ku Klux Klan Kubano targeted West Indian migrant workers and Afro-Cubans.

country_links.evidence_quote: On 16 February 1872, white settlers in the Kingdom of Fiji formed a chapter of the Ku Klux Klan...
country_links.context: White settlers in Fiji formed a chapter of the Ku Klux Klan in 1872.

country_links.evidence_quote: In September 2015, several dozen people rioted in Hennala, Finland... some participants used Ku Klux Klan imagery.
country_links.context: Rioters in Finland used Ku Klux Klan imagery during an attack on a refugee center.

country_links.evidence_quote: In Germany, a KKK-related group called Ritter des Feurigen Kreuzes ('Knights of the Fiery Cross') was founded in 1925 in Berlin...
country_links.context: A KKK-related group called Ritter des Feurigen Kreuzes was founded in Berlin, and later other Klan groups operated in Germany.

country_links.evidence_quote: In Mexico in 1924, a group of vigilantes claimed to have organized themselves into a Klan directed against 'criminals'...
country_links.context: A group of vigilantes in Mexico claimed to have organized themselves into a Klan directed against criminals.

country_links.evidence_quote: A Klan presence has also been reported in the Panama Canal Zone.
country_links.context: A Klan presence has been reported in the Panama Canal Zone.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Navi Clan ('Holy Church of the White Race') was founded in Russia in 1996 and adopted Klan-style robes and rituals...
country_links.context: The Navi Clan was founded in Russia in 1996, adopting Klan-style robes and rituals, and a group calling itself the Russian Ku Klux Klan staged a mock execution.

country_links.evidence_quote: American Klan leader Terry Venable attempted to establish a branch at Rhodes University.
country_links.context: The Klan attempted to establish a branch at Rhodes University in South Africa.

country_links.evidence_quote: Recruitment activity has periodically been reported in the United Kingdom. In the 1960s, klaverns were established in the Midlands...
country_links.context: Klan recruitment activity and klaverns have been reported in the United Kingdom, including in the Midlands, London, and Scotland.
